topic: FPV Drone Building: Detailed Electronics Guide
materials:
## Introduction
FPV (First Person View) drone building combines aeronautics, electronics, and software. This guide walks through selecting components, wiring, and tuning your system.

## Frame Selection
Choose a frame based on motor size, battery capacity, and intended flight style (racing vs cinematic). Common materials include carbon fiber for strength and lightweight.

## Motors and Propellers
Select brushless motors with appropriate KV rating. Pair with propellers that match motor thrust curves. Example: 2205 2300KV motors with 5-inch props for racing.

## ESC (Electronic Speed Controller)
ESCs regulate motor power. Look for 4‑in‑1 units for compact builds. Ensure the ESC current rating exceeds motor draw by at least 20%.

## Flight Controller (FC)
The FC processes sensor data and runs firmware (Betaflight, iNav). Connect IMU, barometer, and optional GPS. Configure PID loops for stable flight.

## Power Distribution
Use a Power Distribution Board (PDB) or solder a custom harness. Maintain proper voltage polarity and keep high‑current wires short to reduce loss.

## Video Transmission
Choose a VTX (Video Transmitter) with adjustable power (25‑800mW). Pair with an appropriate antenna (circular polarized) and ensure compliance with local regulations.

## Receiver and Radio
Select a multi‑protocol receiver (e.g., FrSky R-XSR) compatible with your transmitter. Bind and set failsafe to safe throttle.

## Batteries
LiPo batteries are standard. Verify cell count (3S, 4S, 6S) matches motor and ESC specifications. Observe proper charging and storage procedures.

## Wiring and Soldering Tips
Use heat‑shrink tubing, color‑code wires, and double‑check solder joints. Keep the power leads separate from signal lines to minimize interference.

flashcards.front: What does KV stand for in a brushless motor?
flashcards.back: KV is the motor's RPM per volt (no‑load speed).
It consolidates power distribution and reduces wiring complexity, saving weight and space.
The VTX (Video Transmitter) and its antenna.

questions.text: What is the recommended way to ensure the ESC can handle motor current?
options: Choose an ESC with a current rating at least 10% higher than motor draw.
Select an ESC with a current rating equal to motor draw.
Pick an ESC with a rating 20% higher than the motor's maximum current.
Use any ESC; current rating is not critical.
questions.hint: Add a safety margin to avoid overheating.
questions.explanation: An ESC should be rated at least 20% higher than the motor's maximum current draw to ensure reliable operation and prevent overheating.

questions.text: Which firmware is commonly used on FPV flight controllers?
questions.explanation: Betaflight is the most popular firmware for FPV racing drones, offering extensive tuning and feature support.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of a power distribution board (PDB)?
options: To distribute battery voltage to components
questions.explanation: A PDB distributes the battery voltage to the ESCs, VTX, receiver, and other components, simplifying wiring.
